name: script
defining_feature: Single execution path, no reuse, no callers
one_rule: Readable top-to-bottom in one pass; functions name steps, they don't hide decisions
modules: entry point at top|named functions only for non-trivial transforms|no classes, no DI, no abstracted config|fail loudly, exit nonzero
gate: No abstraction serving exactly one implementation; entry point findable in 5 seconds
width_unit: width 1 — no fan-out, just write it

name: library
defining_feature: Called by external code; the public API is the contract
one_rule: Design from the call site outward: write the ideal calling code first, then implement to satisfy it
modules: explicit public API surface (__all__/exports/pub)|internals hidden behind the API|one module = one responsibility|SemVer if distributed|call-site docs per public function
gate: Every public symbol justified by a caller; no internal type in a public signature; public surface smaller than the implementation
width_unit: one module per worker once the API is frozen

name: data-centric
defining_feature: CRUD with validation; business logic is data-integrity rules
one_rule: Separate by technical role (handler/service/repository) — the domain concepts are too simple to earn a domain model
modules: controller: parse input, call service, format output|service: validate, authorize, orchestrate|repository: encapsulate the data store|model: fields + simple validation
gate: Each function belongs to exactly one layer; calls flow controller->service->repository only; no SQL/ORM in services; no rules in controllers
width_unit: one resource (its controller+service+repository trio) per worker
upgrade_note: Upgrade to domain-rich the moment a service branches on entity state or the same rule appears in two services

name: domain-rich
defining_feature: Complex business rules that exist even without a database
one_rule: Structure mirrors the domain's concepts, not technical layers; the domain layer has zero framework dependencies
modules: domain: entities, value objects, aggregates, domain services, domain events, repository interfaces|application: thin use-case orchestration, transactions, authorization|infrastructure: repository impls, API clients, adapters|interface: controllers, CLI, event handlers
gate: Domain layer imports nothing from infrastructure or interface; rules callable without DB or web server; aggregates enforce their own invariants on every state change
width_unit: one bounded context or aggregate per worker
contract_freeze: aggregate boundaries + repository interfaces + domain event names
upgrade_note: Downgrade to data-centric if domain objects turn out to be mostly getters and setters

name: event-driven
defining_feature: Asynchronous message passing across process boundaries
one_rule: Every handler must be safe to call zero, one, or many times with the same input — idempotency is the price of admission
modules: immutable past-tense events with aggregate ID + correlation ID|imperative commands directed at one handler|one stateless idempotent handler per message type|sagas as explicit state machines|dead-letter queue with payload, error, retry count
gate: Every handler has a documented idempotency strategy; every event schema is versioned; the system survives any single component restarting mid-operation
contract_freeze: event schemas + topic names + idempotency strategy

name: pipeline
defining_feature: Extract-transform-load; the data movement is itself the point
one_rule: Each stage independently testable with a known input schema and output schema; the pipeline is the composition of stages
modules: source/extractor with backpressure and pagination|one transformation per stage, named by what it does|idempotent sink that survives partial failure|explicit error channel preserving failed records
gate: Every stage testable in isolation with canned input; schemas explicit at each boundary; error records preserved, never dropped; replayable from any stage

name: plugin-system
defining_feature: Extension points; core depends on interfaces, plugins implement them
one_rule: The core knows only the interface, plugins know the core — never the reverse
modules: core: extension-point interface + discovery/registration + orchestration, zero plugin imports|minimal contract surface|self-registering plugins|init/execute/dispose lifecycle
gate: Core imports no plugin package; a new plugin lands with zero core changes; a plugin that throws on init cannot crash the core
width_unit: core first as one unit, then one plugin per worker

name: ui-state-flow
defining_feature: UI screens where rendering is a pure function of a single state source; actions/intents up, state down
one_rule: The state-holder is the sole writer of UI state and the sole caller into domain/data; the view is a stateless renderer that only dispatches actions and reads state through selectors
modules: view: stateless renderer, no side effects in render path|state-holder (store/reducer/ViewModel): owns the single UI-state stream per screen|action/intent types: named events with payload shapes|effect layer: async work (network, timers) kept out of reducers|selectors: derived read-only views over state
gate: Every state mutation traces to the state-holder only; each action type has exactly one owning slice; no component holds a hand-synced local copy of store data; reducers/state-holders contain no I/O
width_unit: one screen or state slice (its view + state-holder + action set) per worker
contract_freeze: shared state shape / slice boundaries + action-intent names and payload shapes + navigation routes and their args
upgrade_note: When features multiply past a handful of screens, partition vertically and reclassify as feature-module

name: feature-module
defining_feature: Codebase partitioned vertically by user-facing feature, not horizontally by layer
one_rule: Feature modules may depend on shared core modules, never on each other; cross-feature interaction goes through the app shell (navigation/composition) or a slice public API, never a deep internal import
modules: app shell: composition root + routing/navigation graph|one module per feature: its own view/state/domain/data internals behind one public entry point|core/shared: UI kit, API client, utilities — zero business meaning|entities (web FSD flavor): domain data shapes reusable across features
gate: Zero feature-to-feature internal imports (lint-enforceable); core imports no feature; every feature buildable and testable in isolation
width_unit: one feature module/slice per worker, ownable end-to-end
contract_freeze: each feature public API surface + navigation routes and args + shared core/UI-kit and API-client signatures

name: design-system
defining_feature: A reusable UI-primitive package (tokens + components) with zero knowledge of any consuming app
one_rule: Dependencies point one way: tokens -> atoms -> molecules -> organisms -> app features; the design-system package imports nothing from any app or feature directory
modules: tokens: color/spacing/type/motion as data, never hardcoded values|atoms: smallest reusable primitives|molecules: small compositions with one job|organisms: complex reusable blocks, still business-agnostic|package boundary: versioned, SemVer on breaking prop changes
gate: Zero imports from app/feature code; no component reads app state or calls an app API; every exported prop API documented
width_unit: one component (with variants and stories/tests) per worker, once tokens are frozen
contract_freeze: design tokens + each component public prop API + package versioning contract

name: distributed-service
defining_feature: Multiple independently deployable processes, each owning its data, reachable only over the network
one_rule: A service is reached only through its published network API — never a shared database table, in-process call, or shared mutable library state
modules: ingress/api-gateway|per service: API + domain logic + private datastore|service discovery/registry|published inter-service contracts: versioned API/event schemas, not shared code
gate: No two services share a database schema; zero compile-time dependency between service codebases; every cross-service interaction is a network call or bus message
width_unit: one service (API + logic + datastore) per worker, deployable independently
contract_freeze: each service API/event contract (versioned) + data-ownership boundary (which service is system of record for which entity)
upgrade_note: If services cannot deploy independently or share a DB you have a distributed monolith — collapse back to a modular monolith

name: agent-loop
defining_feature: A host-owned while-loop alternating LLM reasoning and tool execution
one_rule: Host code never branches on domain logic — every post-prompt decision is the model via a tool call; the host only validates and executes the selected tool
modules: loop/runner: while-loop + stop condition + step/cost budget|context store: append-only event history|tool registry: schema-to-handler bindings|tool implementations: one schema + handler + tests each|stop conditions: terminal tool or budget exhaustion
gate: A new tool lands by adding a schema+handler pair with zero edits to the runner; no tool-name special-casing in the loop; an explicit runner-checked stop condition exists
width_unit: one tool (schema + handler + tests) per worker
contract_freeze: the tool-call envelope (name/arguments/return JSON shape) + context-event format + stop-condition protocol
upgrade_note: Upgrade to orchestrator-worker when the task requires runtime decomposition across multiple agent roles

name: orchestrator-worker
defining_feature: A coordinating LLM decomposes tasks at runtime and dispatches isolated worker agents
one_rule: Workers communicate only through task-spec-in / result-out contracts with the orchestrator and never share state, context windows, or direct calls with each other
modules: orchestrator: decomposition + dispatch + synthesis only|worker registry/dispatcher: capability mapping + fan-out control|workers: each a self-contained agent-loop with own prompt, scoped tools, own context|result contracts: structured worker output schemas
gate: Two workers can run concurrently unaware of each other and the merged result is correct from orchestrator-held information alone
width_unit: one worker role (prompt + tool permissions + I/O contract) per builder
contract_freeze: task-spec message format + worker result schemas + capability registry
upgrade_note: Strictly sequential work does not belong here — use a pipeline or prompt chain instead

name: declarative-infra
defining_feature: Infrastructure/deployment desired state declared in a versioned tree and reconciled, never mutated by hand
one_rule: Reusable modules/bases are environment-agnostic; environments are thin parameterizations (tfvars/overlays); cross-module communication happens only through declared inputs and outputs; no out-of-band live mutation
modules: modules/ or base/: single-purpose reusable definitions with explicit variables and outputs|environments/ or overlays/: thin per-env parameterization referencing (not forking) the shared definitions|state/backend or reconciler pointers: one isolated state per environment-layer, one parent pointer per directory
gate: No environment-specific literals inside shared modules; state isolated per environment-layer; every overlay references base via patch/values, never a fork; no hand-applied changes to managed resources
width_unit: one environment-layer instantiation (or one service manifest tree with its overlays) per worker
contract_freeze: module variables/outputs signatures (or base values schema and patch targets) + state backend key naming convention

name: ml-train-serve
defining_feature: ML system with an offline training path and an online serving path that must not drift apart
one_rule: Every feature consumed at serving time is produced by the exact same transformation code that produced it at training time, routed through one feature store
modules: feature pipeline: raw data -> shared transform -> offline + online stores|feature registry: names, types, versions, freshness|training pipeline: point-in-time reads -> train -> evaluate -> registered model|serving path: online store lookups + registered model, no ad-hoc feature code|model registry: versioned, approval-gated artifacts
gate: Exactly one feature-transformation codepath shared by training and serving; deploys come only from the model registry; offline training uses point-in-time-correct retrieval
width_unit: one feature group/view (entity + transform + both materializations) per worker
contract_freeze: entity/join keys and types + feature registry schema + model registry artifact contract
upgrade_note: If there is no online serving path this is just a pipeline — reclassify

name: ecs
defining_feature: Realtime simulation with many entity types sharing composable behaviors
one_rule: Entities are bare IDs, components are pure data, systems are pure per-tick functions over queries — behavior never lives in components and per-entity state never persists inside systems
modules: entity registry: ID allocation only|component stores: per-type contiguous data, no methods|systems: query -> transform functions with declared read/write sets|world/scheduler: owns stores, orders systems, batches structural changes
gate: No logic in component bodies; no per-entity fields cached in systems across ticks; every system declares its component read/write sets
width_unit: one system (or cohesive system group) scoped to a disjoint component read/write set per worker
contract_freeze: component schemas (field layout per type) + each system declared read-set/write-set
